title: The Return Of Avery Jessup

jokes.text: I'm sorry I taste like Korean cigarettes. Tobacco's the only thing with protein over there. Their meat is just deflated kickballs.
jokes.setup: Apology for taste after being held captive
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd comparison of North Korean food to deflated kickballs
jokes.explanation: Perfect character voice for news anchor Avery - clinical reporting style applied to absurd captivity situation. Creative escalation from cigarettes to meat quality.

jokes.text: America's credit rating was downgraded to triple fart-minus
jokes.punchline: Childish 'fart-minus' instead of actual credit rating terminology
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective absurdist news parody. Economic but not particularly character-specific.

jokes.text: Scott Scottsman
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously redundant alliterative name
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurd naming convention. Maximum economy but predictable for the show.

jokes.text: My ears are all plugged up from the plane. Am I talking too loud?
jokes.punchline: Implicit visual of him shouting obliviously
jokes.explanation: Standard visual comedy setup. Well-executed but not original.

jokes.text: And it's me, Johnny Mountain, with the weather on the one and the traffic on the one. You no ask how I do it.
jokes.punchline: Absurd name and broken English delivery with impossible multitasking
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity with distinct character voice. The 'You no ask' delivery adds personality.

jokes.text: And I'm Scott Scottsman... Reminding all of you there is no weekend.
jokes.punchline: Darkly dystopian reminder instead of pleasant goodbye
jokes.explanation: Perfectly subversive news parody. Creates unsettling corporate dystopia in one line.

jokes.text: Or the plant. It's your body.
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemism treating pregnancy like gardening
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Criss's awkwardness with pregnancy talk. Economic delivery.

jokes.text: Right. Well, if I did ever have a... plant with you or whatever... I mean, at my age, we might have to get an Asian plant or accept an older plant with some behavioral issues, and we're a little afraid of it...
jokes.punchline: Increasingly uncomfortable and problematic extension of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon cringe moment - overthinking and making everything worse. Character-specific neurosis.

jokes.text: I mean, that plant's not coming out of your butt, pal, it's coming out of mine.
jokes.punchline: Crude biological reality breaking through euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to plant metaphor - Liz cutting through the euphemism with blunt reality.

jokes.text: The walls are filled with electrocuted mice.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he can't do electrical work
jokes.punchline: Casually horrific building condition
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual delivery of disturbing visual. Great economy and unexpected darkness.

jokes.text: Well, I could dip into my 401K... Is something I've heard old people say in commercials.
jokes.punchline: Reveals she only knows this from commercials, not real life
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing Liz's financial immaturity. Well-structured setup/punchline.

jokes.text: If you take my profits from the hot dog truck and add 'em to the $300 my aunt gave me for Christmas, I'd have $200.
jokes.punchline: Math reveals he's losing money on hot dog business
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Criss's failed entrepreneurship through simple math.

jokes.text: Boy, I sound just like my dad. Which, I guess, makes me Mrs. Lemon. Seriously, though, I gotta get to work. I gotta get to yoga and lunch with the girls.
jokes.setup: Liz worried about sounding like her father
jokes.punchline: Criss immediately acts like a stereotypical housewife
jokes.explanation: Nice gender role reversal humor establishing their dynamic for the episode.

jokes.text: I don't know. There was a lot of Fanta there, and the police, so maybe they co-sponsored it?
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic connecting random wedding elements to sponsorship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - connecting unrelated elements. Character-specific absurdist reasoning.

jokes.text: Ms. Maroney, a wedding is a simple and beautiful ceremony where an old man and a crying girl get pushed into the coupling shed.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing rural/historical wedding description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth line - perfectly captures his disturbing rural background in wedding context. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I know the Southern Tourism Bureau. They pay me a yearly stipend to stay in New York.
jokes.punchline: They pay Tracy NOT to come to the South
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Tracy character integration. Maximum economy with great payoff.

jokes.text: Which is great 'cause our wedding was gonna be slavery-themed anyway.
jokes.setup: Getting married on an old plantation
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning horrifically inappropriate wedding theme
jokes.explanation: Dark Jenna moment showing her obliviousness to appropriateness. Character-specific shock humor.

jokes.text: Of course I am. I have a natural Southern accent, 'cause I'm from le flori-dah pahn-hahndle.
jokes.setup: Claiming to have natural Southern accent
jokes.punchline: Delivers completely artificial, pretentious pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character moment - her pretentiousness undermining her claims of authenticity.

jokes.text: Before moving here, I done used to talk-n-jaw like this, see? And when I'm at work, I use my white-people voice. You should see how we talk in the Bronx. Raar blarg shmoo-boo wagga!
jokes.punchline: Escalating to complete gibberish for Bronx accent
jokes.explanation: Good escalation culminating in absurd gibberish. Kenneth's confused geography is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I heard her ask her bear who the dye job is.
jokes.punchline: Child referring to parent's hair in blunt terms to stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Solid child behavior humor with good economy. Shows realistic child honesty.

jokes.text: There is an iPod 3 and a Mitt Romney 4. They worked all the bugs out. He's not killing hobos at night any more.
jokes.setup: Updating Avery on what she missed
jokes.punchline: Treating politician like tech product with horrific bug fixes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise treating politician like tech upgrade. Dark escalation perfectly executed.

jokes.text: And tomorrow, I am going to throw you a Thanks-Christmas-Valen-Easter-Ween of July party.
jokes.setup: Planning to catch up on missed holidays
jokes.punchline: Absurd compound holiday name
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay escalation. Good character moment showing Jack's over-the-top solutions.

jokes.text: I didn't wear your nightgowns. If they seem stretched out, it's because you're remembering wrong.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to be reassuring about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Awkward defensive statement revealing he did wear them
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Jack's vulnerability through denial. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: I've been writing a sex column for Cosmo. Cosmo is my 14-year-old neighbor. He doesn't know anything.
jokes.setup: Claims expertise writing for Cosmo magazine
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a child neighbor
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Jenna delusion. Well-crafted setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: Sex, money, power, fire, choking, being dragged behind a speed boat... It's all the same thing.
jokes.punchline: Escalating list ending in bizarre specific scenarios
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity perfectly fitting Jenna's dramatic character. The specificity of speedboat adds humor.

jokes.text: And I try to get him pregnant.
jokes.setup: Describing gender role reversal with Paul
jokes.punchline: Impossible biological scenario treated casually
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with maximum economy. Fits Jenna's oblivious character perfectly.

jokes.text: And the best part is, if I want to treat myself to a new pair of Christy LaButtons, I don't have to ask any man for $17.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously low price for designer knockoffs
jokes.explanation: Sets up Jenna's knockoff obsession with good character-specific detail.

jokes.text: I got tired of ruining $1,200 shoes trying to impregnate my boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she buys cheap knockoffs
jokes.punchline: Callback to impossible pregnancy attempt with expensive shoes
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds practical consequences to the absurd premise. Character-consistent logic.

jokes.text: Feminism promised us two things... Fatter dolls, and an end to traditional gender roles.
jokes.punchline: Unexpected and shallow interpretation of feminist goals
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jenna perspective on serious topic. Perfect misdirection with character-specific shallowness.

jokes.text: You wear the pants, Liz. You don't necessarily pull them off because of your hips, but you wear them.
jokes.setup: Encouraging Liz about wearing the pants in relationship
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Liz's appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna backhanded compliment. Well-structured with good character voice.

jokes.text: Am I supposed to just scratch the surface of Channing Tatum's meteoric rise?
jokes.setup: Complaining about one hour to cover whole year
jokes.punchline: Treating minor actor's career as major cultural event
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Liz's misplaced priorities in pop culture.

jokes.text: She's still furious with Al Gore for stealing George Bush's idea to have an Internet.
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards interpretation of internet history
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail showing Avery's conservative delusions. Good political satire.

jokes.text: I have so many missile! I even have nuclear missile. Just for you, sugarprong.
jokes.setup: Jack doing impression of North Korean leader
jokes.punchline: Broken English with inappropriate pet name
jokes.explanation: Standard impression humor with mild offensive stereotyping. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You French-Canadian kissed her mother, which is my name for French kissing when it bums everybody else out.
jokes.punchline: Invented term for gross French kissing
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay with good character voice. Liz's specific and gross terminology is perfect.

jokes.text: Playing psycho-sexual mind games is our normal, Lemon. This is perfect. Everything's perfect.
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting they take time to adjust
jokes.punchline: Jack treating dysfunction as ideal relationship dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - embracing toxicity as romance. Dark but character-accurate.

jokes.text: Dadgum possum up and what been bit my mother's neck brace.
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled grammar and nonsensical sentence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's complete failure at dialect coaching

jokes.text: This isn't Southern elegance. This is a dirtbag girl I once knew. A girl named Jay-nuh! She used to get into fistfights at water parks, and lost her virginity to a bait salesman on a water bed.
jokes.punchline: Specific trashy backstory with escalating details
jokes.explanation: Good escalating specificity creating vivid trashy character. The details are well-chosen.

jokes.text: Kim Jong-Il could not be reached for comment because he was having a totally normal day being alive and not dead.
jokes.punchline: Obvious propaganda denying leader's death
jokes.explanation: Good propaganda parody with obvious awkward denial. Timely political humor.

jokes.text: Our brilliant comrade scored 200 points and then went hot-tubbing with a couple guys he's definitely just friends with.
jokes.punchline: Defensive denial of homosexual implications
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo humor with propaganda context. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You lose, Jack... Is what Kim Jong-Il said to Jack Nicklaus after defeating him in golf today because they're both totally alive.
jokes.setup: Appears to be addressing Jack directly
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's part of propaganda broadcast
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection technique but relies on callback. Well-structured but not standout.

jokes.text: Here's a hint. I like zippers.
jokes.setup: Telling Criss to buy himself something cute
jokes.punchline: Sexual suggestion that's awkward for Liz to make
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Liz attempting sexy talk awkwardly. Economic delivery.

jokes.text: I'm a man. I'm a man, I'm a man! I'm an adult man! You think I like you having to pay for everything? It's humiliating.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate assertions of manhood
jokes.explanation: Good escalating desperation showing fragile masculinity. Character-specific breakdown.

jokes.text: You think I like having to earn Crisspoints so that I can take you on dates that you actually pay for?
jokes.punchline: Reveals bizarre point system for earning date privileges
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist relationship system. Perfect character detail showing power imbalance.

jokes.text: Only because liking the Crisspoints system is one of the ways I earn Crisspoints!
jokes.setup: Liz thinking he liked the point system
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's self-reinforcing trapped system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on relationship dynamics. Perfect escalation showing trapped logic loop. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It is a van with a car engine, Liz, okay?
jokes.setup: Liz calling his hot dog truck a car
jokes.punchline: Defensive technical distinction that doesn't help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Criss's defensive pride over meaningless distinction.

jokes.text: Because you're the person who has to krang that plant for nine months, poop it, and then go back to work to support both of you!
jokes.punchline: Using 'krang' as pregnancy verb plus continuation of plant metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback with creative verb choice. 'Krang' is wonderfully specific and gross.

jokes.text: And he has a watch, and an office with a trash can and a little basketball hoop on it.
jokes.punchline: Pathetically specific office details showing his insecurity
jokes.explanation: Great character specificity. The basketball hoop detail shows Criss's understanding of basic office masculinity.

jokes.text: And yet you're silent about Grant.
jokes.punchline: Liz caught in telling silence about one name
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat humor. Maximum economy with good character insight.

jokes.text: And he plays as hard as he works because, damn it, he deserves to blow off a little steam.
jokes.punchline: Corporate cliché delivered with emotional investment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Criss's aspirational thinking through clichés

jokes.text: Are you crying because there are no roles for actresses in their 40s, nor should there be?
jokes.punchline: Casually misogynistic addition to ageism observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character voice - casually awful addition to already harsh truth. Well-structured.

jokes.text: 50? That's nothing. I once did 100 takes and still couldn't say the word 'incorrigible.'
jokes.punchline: Tracy one-upping with specific pronunciation failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Tracy. The word choice is perfect for his speech patterns.

jokes.text: Great, now I get it! Siri, bring Jessica Tandy back to life.
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking Siri for impossible necromancy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy absurdist logic. Perfect economy and character voice with unexpected escalation.

jokes.text: Jessica Tandy's zombie is coming to find you. Is that correct? Yes, Siri. Thank you, baby.
jokes.punchline: Treating absurd response as normal tech interaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation. Tracy's casual acceptance of zombie threat is perfect character work.

jokes.text: She has the brain of a man, and the ass of a French teenager.
jokes.setup: Describing what he loves about Avery
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate and specific physical comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Jack comment. Character-specific combination of misogyny and creepiness.

jokes.text: Lloyd Blankfein slithers back into the sewers to eat rotten fish at 6:00
jokes.punchline: Treating Goldman Sachs CEO as literal sewer monster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific imagery for Wall Street criticism. Creative and economical with great visual component.

jokes.text: ♪Deathly hallows and Tebow ♪ ♪ Oprah does her last show ♪ ♪ Beyoncé had a baby, yo ♪ ♪ It's a 12-month wrap-up, rap-rap-rap-up ♪
jokes.setup: Liz attempting to summarize year in pop culture
jokes.punchline: Awkward rap with forced rhymes and white-person rhythm
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Liz's awkwardness with hip culture. Character-appropriate failure.

jokes.text: Also, FOX News is right across the street, and word is Greta Van Susteren eats hot dogs like Slimer.
jokes.setup: Describing benefits of hot dog location
jokes.punchline: Specific gross eating comparison to Ghostbusters character
jokes.explanation: Great specific visual comparison. The Slimer reference is perfectly chosen for gross eating habits.

jokes.text: As a Sesame Street purist, I don't think Elmo usually tells passing women that they have a sweet dumper.
jokes.punchline: Contrasting children's show purity with crude street harassment
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition humor. Perfect character detail about Liz being a 'purist' about children's TV.

jokes.text: Legally, I have to say I'm Elmarg!
jokes.setup: Fight with knockoff Sesame Street character
jokes.punchline: Absurd legal disclaimer in middle of street fight
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist legal humor. The timing of disclaimer during violence is perfect.

jokes.text: Them puppets done picked the wrong day to cross my kin! It's on, panhandle style!
jokes.punchline: Reverting to trailer trash fighting mode
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Jenna's suppressed identity. Perfect character escalation under stress.

jokes.text: My outside is shiny and pretty, but my inside is filled with cardboard and horse glue.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest self-assessment using gross materials
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of metaphor. Rare moment of genuine Jenna self-awareness expressed perfectly.

jokes.text: Tonight, I'm Mayor Booo-mberg.
jokes.punchline: Halloween pun on his own name
jokes.explanation: Weak pun humor. Simple wordplay that doesn't add much comedy value.

jokes.text: The woman I kissed is your mother.
jokes.punchline: Shocking revelation about kissing mother-in-law
jokes.explanation: Strong shock humor with good economy. Perfect escalation for Jack's game-playing character.

jokes.text: I had an erotic dream about an adult Dora the explorer. I took her on a balcony in Madrid, above the Plaza Mayor. She had flowers in her hair.
jokes.punchline: Weirdly romantic and specific fantasy about children's character
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable confession with oddly romantic details making it worse

jokes.text: I had an erotic dream about an adult Dora the Explorer. I took her on a balcony in Madrid, above the Plaza Mayor. She had flowers in her hair.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and inappropriate fantasy details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with uncomfortable specificity. The details make it funnier and more cringey.

jokes.text: I thought I was playing you, and you were playing me the whole time. And in 24 hours, I got you to tell me things it would have taken you years to admit.
jokes.setup: Jack thinking he won the mind games
jokes.punchline: Avery revealing she was playing him all along
jokes.explanation: Great character payoff showing Avery outsmarting Jack. Well-structured revelation with good rewatch value.

jokes.text: ♪ J-Lo and Marc Anthony ♪ ♪ Donn and Vicki from O.C. ♪ ♪ 12-month wrap-up, rap-rap-rap-up ♪
jokes.punchline: More forced rhymes about celebrity divorces
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier rap attempt. Shows Liz's persistence with failed approach, character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Johnny Choon, Dolce and Banana, Merolo Blornig, Prader, Gukki, DKNI, Alexandorp McKing, Diana Von Fistenbug
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd corruptions of real designer names
jokes.explanation: Good callback with creative brand name corruptions. Some names are cleverer than others.

jokes.text: Running from your parole officer, attending a theater opening, breaking into your mom's house to steal her cable box.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from formal to criminal activities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation mixing classy and trashy occasions. The specificity of cable box theft adds humor.

tmdb_overview: Avery finally returns home from her kidnapping ordeal in North Korea, yet Jack grows uncertain of her fidelity while she was away. Elsewhere, Criss is crossed due to Liz's role as top wage earner, and Jenna seeks a sponsor for her approaching nuptials.
insight: Avery's return triggers 69 jokes in 41 minutes—escalation comedy drives the season's sharpest finale setup.
